g.text: R = bits used in current 512-bit frame
WARNING: this consumes 1 slot. Only 447 slots remain for zero-fill, not 448.
Zero-fill k bits to end block 1

Off-by-one trap: the 1-bit occupies the 448th slot (index L). If you pad to 448 bits total, the length field has NO room. Target is 448 bits AFTER the 1-bit marker, meaning the marker must land at position <= 447.
L bytes | R=L*8 mod 512 | R+1 <= 448? | blocks | zero bytes k
56 bytes | R=448 | NO (449>448)| 2 | spills to block 2
55->56 byte boundary triggers extra block — the off-by-one trap
k = (511 - R) [fills to end of 512-bit block]
R+1 > 448: no room for 64-bit length field in block 1
